title_en: Methodology and Sources of the Commentary
content_id:
Metodologi Ibn Battal dalam syarahnya atas Sahih al-Bukhari mengikuti konvensi genre yang telah mapan dari syarah klasik Arab, membahas hadis demi hadis di seluruh koleksi al-Bukhari dan menyediakan untuk setiap hadis analisis berlapis-lapis. Pendekatannya ditandai dengan penekanan pada implikasi hukum dari setiap tradisi, mencerminkan latar belakangnya sebagai ahli fikih Maliki dan jabatannya sebagai hakim, daripada pada dimensi linguistik atau biografis dari hadis-hadis tersebut. Penekanan yurisprudensial ini membedakan syarahnya dari karya-karya kemudian yang memberikan perlakuan lebih seimbang terhadap semua aspek syarah hadis.

Salah satu fitur paling bernilai secara historis dari syarah Ibn Battal adalah kutipannya terhadap ulama-ulama awal yang karyanya telah hilang sejak itu. Ia mengandalkan pendapat ulama Maliki abad ketiga dan keempat, pada ahli fikih Irak awal, dan pada tokoh-tokoh dari tradisi keilmuan Andalusia yang nama dan posisinya dilestarikan dalam syarahnya tetapi tidak mudah ditemukan di tempat lain. Pelestarian pendapat keilmuan awal melalui kutipan ini adalah salah satu fungsi terpenting yang dilayani oleh syarah-syarah awal, dan karya Ibn Battal sangat kaya dalam hal ini.

Ibn Battal secara rutin mengutip posisi Malik ibn Anas sendiri, murid-murid utamanya seperti Ibn al-Qasim, dan otoritas Maliki berikutnya di seluruh Afrika Utara dan Andalusia. Ia juga terlibat dengan Abu Hanifah dan tradisi Hanafi, dengan al-Syafi'i, dan dengan posisi Hanbali dalam pertanyaan-pertanyaan hukum yang muncul dari hadis-hadis yang ia bahas. Keterlibatan lintas mazhab yang komprehensif ini lebih menyeluruh dari yang mungkin diharapkan dari seorang hakim Andalusia daerah dan mencerminkan standar tinggi kesarjanaan hukum yang berlaku dalam tradisi Cordoba.

Perlakuan syarah terhadap kritik hadis kurang sistematis dibanding perlakuannya terhadap masalah hukum, mencerminkan prioritas pembentukan pengarangnya. Ibn Battal mencatat ketika suatu sanad telah dinilai lemah oleh otoritas terdahulu tetapi tidak selalu memberikan analisis isnad terperinci yang nantinya akan dijadikan standar oleh para pemberi syarah seperti Ibn Hajar. Bagi para pelajar hadis, syarah ini paling berharga sebagai sumber pendapat hukum awal dan tradisi tafsir awal daripada sebagai panduan untuk pengesahan.
content_en:
Ibn Battal's methodology in his commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari follows the established genre conventions of the classical Arabic commentary (sharh), proceeding hadith by hadith through al-Bukhari's collection and providing for each a multi-layered analysis. His approach is characterized by an emphasis on the legal implications of each tradition, reflecting his training as a Maliki jurist and his service as a judge, rather than on the linguistic or biographical dimensions of the hadiths. This juristic emphasis distinguishes his commentary from later works that provide more balanced treatment of all aspects of hadith commentary.

One of the most historically valuable features of Ibn Battal's commentary is its citation of early scholars whose works have since been lost. He drew on the opinions of third and fourth century Maliki scholars, on early Iraqi jurists, and on figures from the Andalusian scholarly tradition whose names and positions are preserved in his commentary but not easily found elsewhere. This preservation of early scholarly opinion through citation is one of the most important functions that early commentaries serve, and Ibn Battal's work is particularly rich in this respect.

Ibn Battal regularly cites the positions of Malik ibn Anas himself, his major students such as Ibn al-Qasim, and subsequent Maliki authorities across North Africa and Andalusia. He also engages with Abu Hanifa and the Hanafi tradition, with al-Shafi'i, and with the Hanbali positions on legal questions arising from the hadiths he discusses. This comprehensive cross-school engagement is more thorough than one might expect from a provincial Andalusian judge and reflects the high standards of legal scholarship that prevailed in the Córdoba tradition.

The commentary's treatment of hadith criticism is less systematic than its treatment of legal matters, reflecting the priorities of its author's formation. Ibn Battal notes when a chain has been assessed as weak by earlier authorities but does not always provide the detailed isnad analysis that later commentators like Ibn Hajar would make standard. For students of hadith, the commentary is most valuable as a resource for early legal opinion and early interpretive traditions rather than as a guide to authentication.
content_ar: Ibn Battal's methodology in his commentary on صحيح البخاري follows the established genre conventions of the الكلاسيكي Arabic commentary (sharh), proceeding الحديث by الحديث through البخاري's collection and providing for each a multi-layered analysis. His approach is characterized by an emphasis on the legal implications of each التراث, reflecting his training as a المالكي jurist and his service as a judge, rather than on the linguistic or biographical dimensions of the hadiths. This juristic emphasis distinguishes his commentary from later works that provide more balanced treatment of all aspects of الحديث commentary. One of the most historically valuable features of Ibn Battal's commentary is its citation of early العلماء whose works have بما أن been lost. He drew on the opinions of ثالثاً and رابعاً century المالكي العلماء, on early Iraqi jurists, and on figures from the Andalusian العلمي التراث whose names and positions are preserved in his commentary but not easily found elsewhere. This preservation of early العلمي opinion through citation is one of the most المهم functions that early commentaries serve, and Ibn Battal's work is particularly rich in this الاحترام. Ibn Battal regularly cites the positions of مالك ibn أنس himself, his major students مثل Ibn al-Qasim, and subsequent المالكي authorities across North Africa and Andalusia. He أيضاً engages with Abu Hanifa and the الحنفي التراث, with al-الشافعي, and with the الحنبلي positions on legal questions arising from the hadiths he discusses. This comprehensive cross-school engagement is more thorough than one might expect from a provincial Andalusian judge and reflects the high standards of legal scholarship that prevailed in the Córdoba التراث. The commentary's treatment of الحديث criticism is less systematic than its treatment of legal matters, reflecting the priorities of its author's formation. Ibn Battal notes عندما a chain has been assessed as weak by earlier authorities but does not always provide the detailed الإسناد analysis that later commentators like Ibn Hajar would make standard. For students of الحديث, the commentary is most valuable as a resource for early legal opinion and early interpretive traditions rather than as a guide to authentication.
